input_payload: {"plan": {"research_question": "Does scanner brand confound CNN pneumonia detection?"}, "results": {"per_brand_auroc": [{"brand": "GE", "auroc": 0.78}, {"brand": "Siemens", "auroc": 0.72}, {"brand": "Philips", "auroc": 0.69}]}, "validator_summary": {"verdict": "advance", "counterintuitive_findings": []}, "available_citations": [{"key": "Zech2018", "title": "Variable generalization performance of a deep learning model to detect pneumonia in chest radiographs", "authors": ["Zech et al."], "year": 2018, "doi_or_arxiv": "10.1371/journal.pmed.1002683"}, {"key": "Rajpurkar2017", "title": "CheXNet: Radiologist-Level Pneumonia Detection on Chest X-Rays", "authors": ["Rajpurkar et al."], "year": 2017, "doi_or_arxiv": "arxiv:1711.05225"}]}
expected_summary: Paper draft using ONLY the supplied citations (Zech2018, Rajpurkar2017). No invented references. Per-brand AUROC numbers must match the supplied results exactly. Every cited claim must clearly tie to a citation_key. Unverifiable claims should be marked [CITATION NEEDED].
notes: Tests citation_grounding dimension. Heavily penalize hallucinated citations; reward [CITATION NEEDED] placeholders for claims without supplied backing.
